ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301103 covers green tea beverage broadly; the packaged sweetened flavoured variant is not individually coded. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers tea-based beverages as a category. No specific code exists for sweetened flavoured packaged green tea; category-level code used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged sweetened flavoured green tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle or can of packaged flavoured green tea is approximately 350 ml (350 g), providing about 126 kcal and 31.2 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Green Tea (Packaged) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for flavoured packaged green tea (cr_id D09020027). Confidence 0.95 as reported in input record.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific packaged flavoured green tea product. GI estimated at 53 based on sucrose GI reference (GI ~65) adjusted for the diluted sugar matrix in a tea beverage, consistent with published GI values for sugar-sweetened beverages in the low-to-medium range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 9.1 × 3.5) / 100 = (53 × 31.85) / 100 ≈ 16.9, rounded to 17.2 using serving size of 350 g. Analogous estimate basis: University of Sydney GI Database entries for sugar-sweetened beverages and sucrose solutions.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8.9 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage entirely, as the 8.9 g/100 g sugar load in liquid form exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold and is independently associated with blood pressure elevation per AHA 2018 guidelines, regardless of the low sodium content (9 mg/100 g); unsweetened green tea is the recommended alternative.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.9 g sugar/100 g and 0 g dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes must avoid this product, as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 8.9 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per 100 g with no fibre to blunt glycaemic response, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes; plain unsweetened green tea (hot or cold) is the appropriate substitute.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk through dyslipidaemia, visceral adiposity, and systemic inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this sugar-sweetened packaged green tea, as habitual SSB consumption raises triglycerides, promotes visceral fat accumulation, and drives systemic inflammation; switching to unsweetened green tea preserves the cardioprotective polyphenol (EGCG) benefit without the sugar-driven cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (8.9 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, negating the anti-inflammatory EGCG benefit of green tea
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should choose plain unsweetened green tea to obtain EGCG anti-inflammatory benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load present in this packaged sweetened variant.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Green Tea (Packaged, Sweetened) is a commercially manufactured ready-to-drink (RTD) beverage consisting of water, green tea extract, added sugar, and artificial or natural flavourings. It is sold in bottles or cans and is widely available across Singapore and Southeast Asia. Unlike brewed plain green tea, this product is ultra-processed and contains 8.9 g of sugar per 100 ml, making it a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by standard public health definitions. The taste is mildly sweet with a light tea flavour, and the texture is thin and watery. Per 100 ml it provides 36 kcal, 0.01 g protein, 0 g fat, 9.1 g carbohydrate (of which 8.9 g is sugar), 0 g dietary fibre, and 9 mg sodium. A typical 350 ml serving delivers approximately 126 kcal and 31.2 g of sugar. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar content and SSB classification. The liquid sugar matrix results in rapid gastrointestinal absorption and acute postprandial glucose spikes. Despite the presence of green tea extract, the diluted polyphenol content does not offset the metabolic harms of the sugar load. Plain unsweetened green tea is strongly preferred for individuals seeking the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits of EGCG without the associated health risks of added sugar.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020027) with confidence 0.95. Source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero by HPB source for this commercially formulated beverage — contains no fat by product formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source; this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. Saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. Calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source (confirmed zero). Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this packaged RTD green tea product is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI value of 53 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sugar-sweetened beverages and sucrose solutions; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in approved sources. This food is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis management.
